finding_results.notes: Reworked the overwrite aftermath so the scene stays inside touch, muscle memory, panic, and Sarah's misrecognition. Removed the polished moral-accounting language about jargon, deletion, and abstract murder diagnosis.
finding_results.summary: The ending now holds the reader inside the bodily and social theft before landing the final statement that Nate has taken Dominic's place.

finding_results.notes: Sharpened the interface and rollout warnings so occupied targets explicitly require single-subject resolution and reject second-body transport, then echoed that logic again during activation with a partial override banner.
finding_results.summary: The speculative failure mode is now preloaded before blackout: Nate cannot arrive as a second body in an occupied room, so the later overwrite reveal has prior on-page setup.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_01.md:59-63 (first assistant response now terse/procedural — subsystem checklist with no disclaimers, no hedging, same register as any project query); chapters/chapter_01.md:81 (encouraging 'Closer than before' unchanged — already differentiated); chapters/chapter_01.md:99-103 (post-anomaly response now overtechnical — three paragraphs of dense jargon Nate must read three times and translate into 'do it again'); chapters/chapter_01.md:109 (biological-subject escalation now misleadingly practical — assistant gives watts-per-kg specs and treats bio variables as footnoted operating limits rather than issuing warnings); chapters/chapter_01.md:145-153 (machine UI rewritten as settings/config readout — three status lines reading TRANSPORT MODE / TARGET VECTOR / RESOLUTION TYPE instead of five prohibition banners); chapters/chapter_01.md:183 (runtime banner reads as execution status 'SINGLE-SUBJECT RESOLUTION ACTIVE. TARGET VECTOR OCCUPIED.' rather than another cautionary banner); chapters/chapter_01.md:273 (echo phrase 'occupied target vector, consciousness continuity unknown, single-subject resolution' preserved and retroactively reframes the config screen as the warning it actually was)
finding_results.notes: Each of the six assistant/UI interventions now operates in a distinct interaction mode: (1) procedural project checklist, (2) encouraging two-word affirmation, (3) impenetrable technical jargon requiring Nate's translation, (4) helpfully specific engineering specs that bury risk in footnotes, (5) configuration-screen status readout, (6) mid-execution process status. Zero interventions now read as interchangeable calm warnings. The acceptance test requires fewer than three; zero remain. Key structural phrases preserved for the line-273 echo. Escalation pressure shifted into Nate's questions and reactions (line 101: 'Nate read it a third time and got one thing out of it'; line 109: the assistant's helpfulness is the danger) rather than stacking similar warning copy.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_01.md:57; chapters/chapter_01.md:77; chapters/chapter_01.md:83; chapters/chapter_01.md:131; chapters/chapter_01.md:137; chapters/chapter_01.md:209; chapters/chapter_01.md:261
finding_results.notes: Replaced the repeated interpretive follow-up construction with concrete action, bodily response, or cleaner phrasing at each affected beat. The chapter no longer relies on the recurring 'That was...' landing mechanism.
finding_results.summary: Removed the chapter-level prose tic and preserved the same hook, false reassurance, moral ugliness, and disorientation through more specific language.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 1 now preloads the occupied-target overwrite logic during activation and reshapes the final bedside reveal so the consequence lands through bodily panic, trapped proximity, and Sarah calling him Dom rather than through tidy moral explanation.

Single finding (chapter_01_F003) addressed by differentiating six assistant/UI interventions across three spans into distinct interaction modes: terse procedural checklist, dense overtechnical jargon requiring Nate's paraphrase, misleadingly practical spec-sheet language, and config-screen status readouts. The calm-warning cadence that previously unified all interventions is eliminated. Escalation now lives in Nate's questions and his processing of the assistant's responses rather than in repeated warning copy. The chapter's structural echo at line 273 is preserved, gaining irony from the retrospective reframing of configuration text as warning. Chapter length reduced by 11 lines through compression of redundant warning banners. Heading contract, continuity, and character voice preserved.
Addressed the prose/copyedit packet by eliminating the repeated explanatory landing formula in chapter_01 while preserving chapter meaning, pressure, and continuity.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=4, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
